questions.question:
The message 11000101 is to be transmitted using the CRC polynomial x^2 + 1 to protect it from errors. The message that should be transmitted is:
A. 1100010111
B. 1100010110
C. 1100010100
D. 1100010101
questions.to_find: The transmitted bit string after appending CRC check bits to 11000101 using generator x^2 + 1

understand.plain: CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check) is an error-detection method. The sender appends a checksum (remainder) to the original message so that the combined string is exactly divisible by a chosen generator polynomial. The receiver divides the received bits by the same generator; if remainder is zero, no error is detected.

keywords.explain: A generator polynomial used as the divisor in binary polynomial division (mod 2). Its degree determines how many check bits are appended.
keywords.example: x^2 + 1 has degree 2, so 2 check bits are appended.

keywords.explain: Long division where all arithmetic uses XOR (no carries). The remainder after dividing the augmented message by the generator gives the CRC check bits.
The original message with (degree of generator) zeros appended to make room for the check bits.

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 520 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='0' y='0' width='520' height='200' rx='10' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='32' class='lbl ink'>Message: 11000101</text><text x='20' y='60' class='lbl ink'>Generator: x^2+1 = 101 (degree 2)</text><text x='20' y='90' class='sub mut'>Augmented: 11000101 + 00 = 1100010100</text><text x='20' y='116' class='sub mut'>Divide 1100010100 by 101 (mod 2)</text><text x='20' y='142' class='sub ink'>Remainder = 11 (2 check bits)</text><rect x='20' y='155' width='420' height='34' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='30' y='177' class='lbl ink'>Transmitted: 11000101 + 11 = 1100010111</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: CRC steps: original message 11000101 augmented with 00 to form 1100010100, divided by 101 (x^2+1), yielding remainder 11, so transmitted string is 1100010111.

given.aim: Find the transmitted bit string after appending CRC check bits to the message 11000101 using generator polynomial x^2 + 1.

terms.connects: Augmented by appending 2 zeros before division.

terms.meaning: 3-bit divisor polynomial; degree 2 means 2 check bits.
terms.connects: Used as the divisor in mod-2 polynomial long division.

terms.meaning: Message with (degree of G) = 2 zeros appended to the right.
terms.connects: The dividend in the CRC division step.

terms.meaning: The result of (augmented message) mod G; these become the check bits.
terms.connects: Appended to the original message to form the transmitted string.

given.plan: Append 2 zeros to the message to get the augmented message 1100010100. Perform mod-2 polynomial long division of 1100010100 by 101. The 2-bit remainder is appended to the original message to form the transmitted string.

steps.title: Form the augmented message
steps.apply:
Generator x^2 + 1 has degree 2. Append 2 zeros to message:
11000101 -> 1100010100 (10 bits).
steps.note: The number of appended zeros equals the degree of the generator polynomial.

steps.title: Mod-2 long division: 1100010100 / 101
steps.formula_raw: XOR each 3-bit window with 101 when the leading bit is 1
steps.apply:
Working through the bits step by step:
1. Bits 0-2: 110 XOR 101 = 011; bring down bit 3 (0): window = 0110
2. 011x -> shift (leading 0): bring down bit 4 (0): window = 1100... actually tracking the full stream:
   Start: 1100010100
   XOR 101 at pos 0: 0110010100
   XOR 101 at pos 1: 0011010100
   XOR 101 at pos 2: 0001110100
   XOR 101 at pos 3: 0000100100
   XOR 101 at pos 4 (skip leading 0s): 0000001100... wait at pos 5? No, pos 4 gives 000 0 1->XOR: 0000001100... 
   Tracking carefully:
   Step by step XOR (at each position where bit=1):
   1100010100 XOR (101 at pos 0): pos0=1^1=0,pos1=1^0=1,pos2=0^1=1 -> 0110010100
   0110010100 XOR (101 at pos 1): pos1=1^1=0,pos2=1^0=1,pos3=0^1=1 -> 0011010100
   0011010100 XOR (101 at pos 2): pos2=1^1=0,pos3=1^0=1,pos4=0^1=1 -> 0001110100
   0001110100 XOR (101 at pos 3): pos3=1^1=0,pos4=1^0=1,pos5=1^1=0 -> 0000100100
   0000100100 XOR (101 at pos 6): pos6=0,not 1; check pos4=1: XOR at pos4: pos4=1^1=0,pos5=0^0=0,pos6=0^1=1 -> 0000001100
   0000001100 XOR (101 at pos 6): pos6=1^1=0,pos7=1^0=1,pos8=0^1=1 -> 0000000110
   0000000110 XOR (101 at pos 7): pos7=1^1=0,pos8=1^0=1,pos9=0^1=1 -> 0000000011
   pos 8 and 9 remain: the remainder is 11 (2 bits, degree < 2).
steps.note: At each step, if the current leading bit is 1, XOR the next 3 bits with 101; otherwise skip. Repeat until fewer than 3 bits remain.

steps.title: Form the transmitted string
steps.formula_raw: T = M + R = original message + remainder
steps.apply:
Remainder R = 11. Append to original message:
T = 11000101 + 11 = 1100010111 (10 bits).

questions.formula_note: CRC computation: augment message with (degree of generator) zeros, perform mod-2 polynomial long division, append the remainder as check bits to the original message.

questions.question:
A computer network uses polynomials over GF(2) for error checking with 8 bits as information bits and uses x^3 + x + 1 as the generator polynomial to generate the check bits. In this network, the message 01001011 is transmitted as:
A. 01001011010
B. 01001011100
C. 01001011110
D. 01001011000
questions.to_find: The full transmitted bit string (information bits + CRC check bits) for message 01001011 using generator x^3 + x + 1

understand.plain: This question applies CRC error detection. Given an 8-bit message and a degree-3 generator polynomial, 3 check bits are computed and appended to the message. The generator x^3 + x + 1 in binary is 1011. The augmented message (with 3 zeros appended) is divided by 1011 using mod-2 arithmetic. The 3-bit remainder forms the check bits.

keywords.explain: Galois Field of size 2; arithmetic is done with bits 0 and 1 where addition = XOR and multiplication = AND. CRC uses polynomial arithmetic over GF(2).
The divisor polynomial used for CRC. Its degree determines the number of check bits. x^3 + x + 1 has degree 3, so 3 check bits are generated.
keywords.example: x^3 + x + 1 -> binary 1011 (coefficients of x^3, x^2, x^1, x^0).

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 540 210' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='0' y='0' width='540' height='210' rx='10' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='32' class='lbl ink'>Information bits: 01001011 (8 bits)</text><text x='20' y='58' class='lbl ink'>Generator: x^3+x+1 = 1011 (degree 3)</text><text x='20' y='84' class='sub mut'>Augmented: 01001011 + 000 = 01001011000 (11 bits)</text><text x='20' y='110' class='sub mut'>Divide 01001011000 by 1011 (mod 2 polynomial division)</text><text x='20' y='136' class='sub ink'>Remainder = 100 (3 check bits)</text><rect x='20' y='150' width='480' height='44' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='30' y='168' class='lbl ink'>Transmitted:</text><text x='30' y='188' class='sub ink'>01001011 + 100 = 01001011100 (11 bits)</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: CRC steps: message 01001011 augmented to 01001011000, divided by 1011, remainder is 100, transmitted string is 01001011100.

given.aim: Compute the 3 CRC check bits for message 01001011 using generator x^3 + x + 1, and form the transmitted 11-bit string.

terms.connects: Augmented by appending 3 zeros before CRC division.

terms.meaning: 4-bit divisor (degree 3); used in mod-2 polynomial division.
terms.example: Coefficients of x^3=1, x^2=0, x^1=1, x^0=1 -> 1011.
terms.connects: The divisor; its degree (3) determines how many zeros to append.

terms.meaning: Message with 3 zeros appended (degree of G = 3).
terms.connects: The dividend in mod-2 polynomial long division.

terms.meaning: The 3-bit CRC check bits obtained after dividing augmented message by G.
terms.connects: Appended to the original message to form the 11-bit transmitted string.

given.plan: Append 3 zeros to message to form 01001011000. Perform mod-2 polynomial long division by 1011. Read the 3-bit remainder. Append it to the original message to get the transmitted string.

steps.title: Form the augmented message
steps.apply:
Generator has degree 3. Append 3 zeros:
01001011 -> 01001011000 (11 bits).
steps.note: Leading zero is fine; polynomial division still works.

steps.title: Mod-2 long division: 01001011000 / 1011
steps.formula_raw: XOR 4-bit window with 1011 whenever leading bit = 1
steps.note: The remainder has degree < 3 (3 bits), which is correct.

steps.title: Construct the transmitted string
steps.apply:
Remainder R = 100. Append to original message:
T = 01001011 || 100 = 01001011100 (11 bits).

questions.formula_note: CRC over GF(2): augment message with k zeros (k = degree of generator), divide by generator using mod-2 polynomial long division, append the k-bit remainder as check bits.

questions.question:
Consider the cyclic redundancy check (CRC) based error detecting scheme having the generator polynomial x^3 + x + 1. Suppose the message 10000101 is to be transmitted. Check bits are appended at the end of the message by the transmitter using the above CRC scheme. The transmitted bit string is denoted by T. The value of the check bits c_2c_1c_0 is:
A. 101
B. 110
C. 100
D. 111
questions.to_find: The 3 CRC check bits c_2c_1c_0 appended to message 10000101 using generator x^3 + x + 1

understand.plain: The transmitter uses CRC to compute 3 check bits for message 10000101. The generator x^3 + x + 1 = 1011 has degree 3. The message is augmented with 3 zeros and divided by 1011 using mod-2 polynomial arithmetic. The 3-bit remainder is the CRC, and those bits become c_2c_1c_0 which are appended to the message.

keywords.explain: The 3-bit CRC remainder appended to the message. c_2 is the most significant check bit, c_0 is the least significant.
keywords.example: If remainder = 111, then c_2=1, c_1=1, c_0=1.

keywords.explain: Binary long division where subtraction = XOR. No borrows or carries. The remainder after dividing the augmented message by the generator gives the CRC.

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 540 220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='0' y='0' width='540' height='220' rx='10' class='bg'/><text x='20' y='32' class='lbl ink'>Message: 10000101 (8 bits)</text><text x='20' y='58' class='lbl ink'>Generator: x^3+x+1 = 1011 (degree 3)</text><text x='20' y='84' class='sub mut'>Augmented: 10000101 + 000 = 10000101000 (11 bits)</text><text x='20' y='110' class='sub mut'>Divide 10000101000 by 1011 (mod 2)</text><text x='20' y='136' class='sub ink'>Remainder = 111 -> c_2c_1c_0 = 111</text><rect x='20' y='150' width='490' height='56' rx='6' class='hi'/><text x='30' y='172' class='lbl ink'>Transmitted T = 10000101111</text><text x='30' y='196' class='sub ink'>Check: 10000101111 / 1011 = 0 remainder (verified)</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Message 10000101 augmented to 10000101000, divided by 1011 mod-2, remainder = 111, so c_2c_1c_0 = 111 and transmitted string T = 10000101111.

given.aim: Compute the 3 CRC check bits for message 10000101 using generator polynomial x^3 + x + 1 = 1011.

terms.connects: Augmented with 3 zeros before dividing by the generator.

terms.meaning: 4-bit divisor polynomial of degree 3; determines 3 check bits.
terms.connects: The divisor in CRC division; degree tells how many zeros to append.

terms.meaning: Original message with 3 zeros appended to right (for degree-3 generator).
terms.connects: The 11-bit dividend for mod-2 polynomial long division.

terms.meaning: 3-bit result of (augmented message) mod G; these are the check bits.
terms.connects: Appended to original message to form transmitted string T.

given.plan: Append 3 zeros to message to get 10000101000. Perform mod-2 polynomial long division by 1011 step by step. Read the 3-bit remainder as c_2c_1c_0.

steps.title: Form the augmented message
steps.formula_raw: Augmented = M appended with (degree of G) zeros
steps.apply:
Generator has degree 3. Append 3 zeros:
10000101 -> 10000101000 (11 bits, positions 0-10).
steps.note: This ensures the dividend has enough room for the check bits to be extracted as the remainder.

steps.title: Mod-2 long division: 10000101000 / 1011
steps.formula_raw: At each position i: if bit i = 1, XOR bits i through i+3 with 1011
steps.note: Remainder has exactly 3 bits (degree < 3 as required). c_2=1, c_1=1, c_0=1.

steps.title: Verify the transmitted string
steps.formula_raw: T = M || R; T / G must give remainder 0
steps.apply:
T = 10000101 || 111 = 10000101111.
Verification: dividing 10000101111 by 1011:
  Steps produce final remainder 000, confirming no errors in computation.
steps.note: The check bits 111 are c_2c_1c_0. If the receiver gets T = 10000101111 and divides by 1011, they get remainder 000 (no error detected).

solution.result: The check bits c_2c_1c_0 = 111 (Option D). The transmitted string is T = 10000101111.

questions.formula_note: CRC computation: augment message with k zeros, divide by degree-k generator polynomial using mod-2 (XOR-based) long division, the k-bit remainder is the CRC appended as check bits.
